content: Hi, I'm Devon, nice to meet you. I've been dealing with chronic back pain and I'm looking for ways to manage it using technology.
Nice to meet you too, Devon. I'm sorry to hear about your back pain. What kind of technology have you been using so far to manage your pain?
I've been using a fitness tracker to monitor my activity levels and a mindfulness app to help with stress relief. I've also been seeing a physical therapist at a clinic in downtown Boston.

Those are great tools to have. Mindfulness and stress relief can be very helpful in managing chronic pain. Have you considered any other tech tools, such as a pain tracking app or a wearable device that can monitor your posture?
I haven't yet, but I'm open to trying new things. I've been trying to stay active despite my back pain, I've been doing some urban sketching and going for short walks.
Urban sketching is a great hobby, it can be very therapeutic. Being active is also important for managing chronic pain. What kind of sketching do you like to do?
I like to sketch buildings and landscapes. I've also been trying to get into marathon running, but my back pain has been holding me back.
Marathon running is a big goal, don't give up on it. Have you considered working with a running coach or using a running app to help you train safely and manage your pain?
I haven't yet, but that's a great idea. I've been doing some research on different running shoes and I'm thinking of investing in a new pair.
New running shoes can make a big difference. What's your current favorite brand or model?
I've been using Brooks, but I'm thinking of trying out a new brand. I've also been hiking in the mountains near my home in Colorado, it's great exercise and a beautiful way to clear my mind.

Colorado is a beautiful state, the mountains are stunning. Hiking is a great way to stay active and enjoy nature. Be sure to take care of your back and listen to your body, especially when hiking or running.
I will, thanks for the advice. I'm actually planning a trip to the Rockies next month, I'm really looking forward to it.
The Rockies are a great destination, you'll have a wonderful time. Don't forget to pack your fitness tracker and running shoes, and to stay safe while hiking.
